title: Game Loop

content.heading: Putting it together — the game loop

body.text: The light-up grid game has four functions that call each other in a cycle. Understanding this cycle is the difference between reading code and understanding it.

rows: Picks cells, lights them up, sets a timer
Records player click, checks if done

body.label: This is the full game
body.text: These four functions plus four state variables are the entire game logic. Everything else is presentation.

exercises.title: Write the game loop in isolation
tasks: Write all four state variables at the top: litCells, playerClicks, isInputAllowed, level.
Write startRound(): reset playerClicks, set isInputAllowed false, pick 'level' random numbers from 0-8, store in litCells, log them, then after 1500ms log 'Lights off' and set isInputAllowed true.
Write checkResult(): compare sorted arrays. If match: level++, log 'Correct! Level: ' + level, call startRound after 1000ms. If not: level=2, log 'Wrong.', call startRound after 1500ms.
Call startRound() once to test the flow in the console.
This is the full game logic — no DOM required to verify it works.
exercises.hint: For picking random numbers without duplicates, use the pickCells pattern from Session 4: start with all indexes, pick and splice one at a time.

checks.label: pickCells(2) returns an array of length 2

exercises.title: Debug the input guard
tasks: The code below has a bug: clicks register even during the display phase.
Trace through it and find the single line that is wrong.
Fix it so clicks are ignored while isInputAllowed is false.
Confirm by reading the log output — clicks during display phase should log 'Blocked'.
exercises.hint: Look at the condition in handleClick. The logic is inverted.

customize: Change the display duration from 1500ms to 2000ms for an easier game.
Change it to 800ms for a harder game.
Change the starting level from 2 to 3 so the first round shows 3 cells.
Add a console.log at the start of each round showing the current level.
